title: Short insert size WGS libraries preparation for assembly of the Lateolabrax maculatus genome
author: Chang Li
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.sszeef6

steps.instruction: The extracted DNA was sheared into fragments between 50 bp and ~800 bp in size using a Covaris E220 ultrasonicator (Covaris, Brighton, UK). Treat time 20s, Acoustic Duty Factor 25%, Peak Incident Power 500W, Cycles Per Burst 500, 24 cycles. 00:00:20
Fragments between 150 bp and 250 bp or 200 to 500 bp were selected using AMPure XP beads (Agencourt, Beverly, the U.S.). AMPure XP beads Beckman Coulter

Repaired using T4 DNA polymerase, (ENZYMATICS, Beverly, the U.S.) 30 min. at 20 ℃ to obtain blunt ends which were then 3’-adenlyated to create sticky ends. T4 DNA polymerase Enzymatics 00:30:00 Undetermined
These DNA fragments were ligated at both ends to T-tailed adapters and amplified.
The temperature profile was 3 min. at 95 ℃ followed by 8 cycles of 20 sec. at 98 ℃, 15 sec. at 60 ℃, 30 sec. at 72 ℃, and more 10 min. at 72 ℃ for further elongation.
AMPure XP beads (Agencourt, Beverly, the U.S.) was used to purify the PCR production.
